outlook: UX design roles have contracted sharply, with open positions dropping 44% to 12,614 over the past year as companies tighten hiring after years of rapid expansion. The $111,000 median salary reflects the field's continued value, but moderate AI risk emerges from tools like Figma AI, Galileo AI, and Uizard that are beginning to automate wireframing and prototyping tasks. Community discussions focus more on AI integration than layoffs, suggesting designers are actively adapting their workflows rather than facing wholesale displacement. The field appears to be stabilizing at a new baseline after the post-pandemic hiring surge, with AI tools reshaping how UX work gets done rather than eliminating the need for strategic design thinking.

skillPivots.headline: Master Figma's advanced prototyping capabilities
skillPivots.body: Since Figma AI is a primary displacing tool, become an expert in Figma's complex interaction design, component systems, and developer handoff features. Position yourself as the designer who can leverage AI assistance while creating sophisticated, production-ready designs that require human strategic thinking.

skillPivots.headline: Develop product strategy and research skills
skillPivots.body: With declining UX demand, pivot toward product strategy by combining your design background with user research methodologies, competitive analysis, and business metrics. This positions you as a strategic product thinker rather than just a wireframe creator.

skillPivots.headline: Lead cross-functional design system initiatives
skillPivots.body: As AI tools like Uizard and Galileo AI democratize basic design creation, focus on building and governing enterprise design systems that require deep technical knowledge. Become the expert who ensures design consistency and quality across teams using various AI-assisted tools.
